Tianyi Xie
  • Home
  • Experience

Research

Short-Term Feedback, Learning, and Investor Rationality

With Prof. Raghavendra Rau
Sep. 2025

A controlled-field hybrid experiment studying how short-term investment outcomes shape judgment precision, risk behavior, and adaptive learning.

  • Designed a controlled-field hybrid experiment to evaluate how short-term investment outcomes shape judgment precision and risk behavior.
  • Implemented randomized feedback sequences and collected real-world account aggregates to measure calibration, aggression, and attribution patterns.
  • Conducted longitudinal analysis revealing dynamic shifts from emotionally driven reactions to experience-based adaptive learning.
  • Identified mechanisms linking recency effects, overconfidence, and bounded learning; developed low-cost behavioral interventions for trading platforms.
  • Worked on a paper about whether short-term experience enhances the accuracy of investors' decisions. [Paper Link]

Course Projects

Modeling Collision Probability at Scale: A Simulation Study of Threshold Effects (Quant / Math)

Mar. 2026

A scalable simulation framework for studying distributional dynamics, convergence behavior, and threshold effects in discrete probabilistic systems.

  • Designed and implemented a scalable simulation framework to investigate distributional dynamics and threshold phenomena in discrete probabilistic systems.
  • Integrated theoretical derivations with large-scale numerical experiments to validate convergence properties and reveal nonlinear growth patterns underlying the classic Birthday Paradox.
  • Reframed the paradox as a broader class of collision probability modeling problems, enabling practical insights into hashing algorithms, data deduplication pipelines, and large-scale distributed infrastructure.
  • Demonstrated how probabilistic modeling and simulation can inform system design decisions where collision risks directly impact performance, reliability, and storage efficiency.

Skills: Monte Carlo simulation; Probabilistic modeling; Threshold analysis; System risk modeling

Causal Inference Analysis of Voter Mobilization Experiments

Oct. 2025

This project analyzes large-scale voter mobilization experiments using causal inference methods to estimate treatment effects and behavioral response patterns.

  • Combined statistical modeling with empirical evaluation to investigate how different outreach interventions influence voter turnout across heterogeneous demographic groups.
  • Emphasized identification strategies, robustness checks, and interpretation of policy-relevant causal effects in large-scale experimental settings.
  • Demonstrated how data-driven experimentation and causal reasoning can inform decision-making in political campaigns, marketing interventions, and public policy design where understanding behavioral impact is critical.

Skills: Causal inference; Statistical modeling; Experimental evaluation; Behavioral data analytics

Southeast Asian Economy - Strategic Analysis of Xiaomi’s Business Model (Business / Econ)

Mar. 2025

This project investigates Xiaomi's strategic expansion in Southeast Asian markets by integrating macroeconomic analysis, competitive benchmarking, and business strategy frameworks.

  • Using structured analytical tools such as Porter's Five Forces and value-chain analysis, the study evaluates Xiaomi's transition from a cost-efficient smartphone manufacturer to an ecosystem-driven technology platform.
  • The analysis further explores how regional factors including demographic growth, income distribution, and smartphone penetration influence consumer adoption and market competition.
  • Based on these insights, the project proposes strategic recommendations related to pricing optimization, supply-chain leverage, and long-term brand positioning in emerging markets.

Skills: Market analysis; Competitive benchmarking; Economic interpretation; Strategic frameworks

Last updated: Mar 2026
